message:
So last week we had a water main break two streets over, and suddenly our neighborhood group chat went absolutely wild. My sister texted 'Do we have water??' and my mom replied with a photo of our emergency supply like it was a trophy 😅

Turns out we were fine, but it got me thinking — how many of us actually know what's in our own homes? I realized I couldn't tell you off the top of my head where our documents are stored or how much water we keep on hand. (Spoiler: I fixed that.)

The funny part? Once I actually sat down and made a simple list of what we had and what we needed, I felt SO much calmer. No drama, no spending spree — just... real.

Do you and your family ever actually talk about this stuff, or is it one of those things everyone assumes someone else is handling? 🙂
